# UC-23.7.1 · Support Ticket Volume and Resolution SLA Dashboard

## Description

Shows support ticket volume, backlog, and SLA compliance in real time — how many tickets are open, how fast they're being resolved, and whether the team is meeting response and resolution targets. Support leaders see whether they need to add staff, redistribute workload, or investigate a spike in a specific issue category.

## Value

Shows support ticket volume, backlog, and SLA compliance in real time — how many tickets are open, how fast they're being resolved, and whether the team is meeting response and resolution targets. Support leaders see whether they need to add staff, redistribute workload, or investigate a spike in a specific issue category.

## Implementation

(1) Configure ServiceNow TA for incident ingestion; (2) map your SLA targets by priority; (3) schedule every 4 hours for team leads; (4) alert when any team's backlog exceeds capacity threshold; (5) add first-response time tracking alongside resolution time.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, priority, assignment_group, category, short_description).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Configure ServiceNow TA for incident ingestion; (2) map your SLA targets by priority; (3) schedule every 4 hours for team leads; (4) alert when any team's backlog exceeds capacity threshold; (5) add first-response time tracking alongside resolution time.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval resolution_hours=if(isnotnull(closed_epoch), round((closed_epoch-opened_epoch)/3600, 1), null())
| eval sla_target_hours=case(priority="1",4, priority="2",8, priority="3",24, 1=1,72)
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(resolution_hours) AND resolution_hours <= sla_target_hours, 1, 0)
| eval is_open=if(isnull(closed_at) OR state IN ("New","In Progress","On Hold"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_tickets,
        sum(is_open) as open_tickets,
        sum(sla_met) as within_sla,
        avg(resolution_hours) as avg_resolution_h,
        median(resolution_hours) as median_resolution_h by assignment_group
| eval sla_pct=round(100*within_sla/(total_tickets-open_tickets), 1)
| sort - open_tickets
| table assignment_group, total_tickets, open_tickets, avg_resolution_h, median_resolution_h, sla_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Support Ticket Volume and Resolution SLA Dashboard** — Shows support ticket volume, backlog, and SLA compliance in real time — how many tickets are open, how fast they're being resolved, and whether the team is meeting response and resolution targets. Support leaders see whether they need to add staff, redistribute workload, or investigate a spike in a specific issue category.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (number, opened_at, closed_at, state, priority, assignment_group, category, short_description). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **closed_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **resolution_hours** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_target_hours** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_met** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **is_open** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by assignment_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Support Ticket Volume and Resolution SLA Dashboard**): table assignment_group, total_tickets, open_tickets, avg_resolution_h, median_resolution_h, sla_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare ticket counts, times, and SLA results to the support tool (Zendesk, Freshdesk, Intercom, ServiceNow) and to any WFM or ACD views your team already trusts for the same queues and dates.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (open backlog, SLA %), Bar chart (volume by team), Line chart (daily ticket trend), Table (team performance).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d
| eval opened_epoch=strptime(opened_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed_epoch=if(isnotnull(closed_at), strptime(closed_at, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), null())
| eval resolution_hours=if(isnotnull(closed_epoch), round((closed_epoch-opened_epoch)/3600, 1), null())
| eval sla_target_hours=case(priority="1",4, priority="2",8, priority="3",24, 1=1,72)
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(resolution_hours) AND resolution_hours <= sla_target_hours, 1, 0)
| eval is_open=if(isnull(closed_at) OR state IN ("New","In Progress","On Hold"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_tickets,
        sum(is_open) as open_tickets,
        sum(sla_met) as within_sla,
        avg(resolution_hours) as avg_resolution_h,
        median(resolution_hours) as median_resolution_h by assignment_group
| eval sla_pct=round(100*within_sla/(total_tickets-open_tickets), 1)
| sort - open_tickets
| table assignment_group, total_tickets, open_tickets, avg_resolution_h, median_resolution_h, sla_pct
```

## Visualization

Single value (open backlog, SLA %), Bar chart (volume by team), Line chart (daily ticket trend), Table (team performance).
